data.title: Dividend Harvesting Portfolio Week 207: $20,700 Allocated, $2,010.09 In Projected Dividends
data.description:
2025 continues to be an exciting year as the S&P 500 climbed 1.06% while the Nasdaq increased by 1.74% this week. We're only 2 weeks into February, and the S&P 500 is already up 3.96% YTD, and my prediction that the market will reach 7,000 by the end of 2025 doesn't look as crazy as it did during the end of 2024. If the market climbs another 15% this year, the S&P will exceed 7,000, and we will have 3 more earnings seasons before the end of 2025. CPI came in a bit hotter than expected, but it didn't derail the market. I think the critical thing to look at is that the index for shelter rose 0.4%, accounting for nearly 30% of the increase in CPI. The FOMC minutes come out this week, and it will be interesting to see what they discussed. I don't believe the Fed is only going to cut 2 times in 2025, as the only way to get shelter down at this point is to lower rates. We need an environment where the cost of capital is lower, and landlords can refinance at lower rates. If that doesn't happen, I do not see a solution to stabilize or lower rents. I am confident that the economy will be strong in 2025, and it will be interesting to see what President Trump announces as part of his tax plan for corporations. I think there are still a lot of opportunities in the market, and sectors such as REITs should do well as rates trend lower.

At some point, the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio will experience a retracement, but until then, I am going to enjoy these types of weeks. In week 207, the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio reached a new milestone in terms of the total account value and amount of dividend income being generated. After allocating $20,700 to the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio, the account balance finished in week 207 at $26,630.67. The account's profitability increased by $233.81 to $5,930.67, which is a return on invested capital of 28.65%. This week, I added to my positions in Pfizer Inc. (PFE), Starwood Property Trust, Inc. (STWD), and the Reaves Utility Income Trust (UTG). The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio generated $16.76 of dividend income in week 207. The combination of reinvesting the generated dividend income and making these investments increased the forward projected annualized dividend income by $8.60 (0.43%) to $2,010.09. New milestones are being reached, and I think that 2025 will be an exceptional year for the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio as I continue to allocate capital and reinvest all the dividends that are produced.

The Overall Performance of the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio

Since week 1, I have stuck to my investment rules of investing $100 per week and not letting any positions exceed 5% of the portfolio or any sector exceed 20% of the portfolio. This has forced me to allocate my invested capital across many positions, which, I believe, has helped me achieve my investment goals of generating recurring income while mitigating downside risk. Capital appreciation is a secondary goal that I am trying to generate as a byproduct of my first 2 goals. When I look at the chart below, I see that this strategy has allowed me to withstand macroeconomic headwinds, geopolitical tensions, and a rising rate environment quite well. The portfolio is now up $5,930.67 (28.65%) and is generating $2,010.09 in forward dividend income, which is a yield of 9.71% on invested capital and 7.55% on the current portfolio value. I am pleased with the portfolio structure and believe it will continue to appreciate in value as 2025 progresses.

The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio Dividend Section

Here's how much dividend income is generated per investment basket:

  • ETFs $577.09 (28.71%).
  • Equities $511.69 (25.46%).
  • REITs $355.63 (17.69%).
  • CEFs $335.21 (16.68%).
  • BDCs $230.48 (11.47%).

Collecting dividends can serve many functions in a portfolio. Some investors utilize dividends to supplement their income and live off of them. I'm building a dividend portfolio for myself 30 years into the future. In 2022, I collected $507.80 in dividend income from 533 dividends. In 2023, I collected $978.11 in dividend income from 660 dividends. 2024 was a great year as the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio generated $1,623.14 in dividend income from 709 dividends. After 7 weeks into 2025, $309.11 of dividend income was produced, which is 19.04% of the total income from 2024. There were 88 positions that paid a dividend, which is 12.41% of the dividends generated in 2024.

When I look back at the earlier years, I find it amazing how much progress has been made. In the first 7 weeks of 2025, I averaged $44.16 in dividend income. This is an increase of 41.47% or $12.94 compared to my weekly average of $31.21 in 2024. At this point last year, I had generated $163.59 compared to $309.11. I am very excited to get through Q1 so I can do a YoY comparison, as it will help me model out what to expect throughout the remainder of 2025. I do think my original goals for 2025 may have been a bit conservative, and after Q1, I may be updating them depending on what the data indicates.

We're halfway through February, and I have already produced $71.53 in dividend income, which is 73.77% of the total dividend income generated last February. A lot of dividend income is generated at the end of the month due to a lot of monthly CEFs and ETFs being paid. I have no idea where the final number will land, but I expect it to be north of $125. This will be a good indication of what will occur next month, as February and March were the only 2 months in 2024 where I didn't exceed $100 of dividend income. I am still a bit shocked that I already exceeded $200 in January, and there is certainly a chance that the largest month for 2025 could exceed $225 or $250 of dividend income.

This week, the NEOS NASDAQ-100(R) High Income ETF (QQQI) increased by 1.59%, which caused it to no longer generate at least 1 share of its dividend income. There are now 39 positions generating at least 1 share per year from their dividends, and the additional shares are expected to produce $175.52 in additional dividend income. I have started working on some positions that are not in the green section of the table below, and I need to try to allocate at least 50% of the capital I am deploying each week to these positions so I can round out the portfolio further. I knew that I would be a bit top-heavy, and while I am not exceeding a 5% allocation toward a single position, I want to diversify further into my smaller positions so they generate at least 1 share per year from their dividends.

The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio Composition

ETFs and REITs are still under 20% of the portfolio, but I can see either sector exceeding 20% for a minimal amount of time. After doing some research, I found that there are several REITs I want to increase my exposure to because I feel they are very undervalued. In the next several weeks, I will probably increase my exposure to the oil & gas and technology sectors as there are some interesting opportunities in those spaces. Individual equities now represent 37.73% of the Dividend Harvesting portfolio while generating 25.46% of the dividend income. REITs, ETFs, CEFs, and BDCs make up 62.27% of the portfolio and generate 74.54% of the forward income. I am working on getting individual equities to represent more of the portfolio. The long-term goal is to have the sectors be close to equal weight, but that may not happen for 5–10 years at this rate, if ever.

The top-10 positions continue to decline a bit, but I still want Altria Group, Inc. (MO) to get under 4% of the portfolio. I think that it's a big accomplishment that none of the positions are close to 5%, and only 4 positions exceed 3% of the portfolio. I am happy that the allocations are becoming more even when I look at the Top 20 positions. One of my long-term goals is to get this chart to flatten out further. That way, it will be easier for me to make investments in larger positions in the future. I think there are a lot of opportunities throughout the portfolio and in the top 20 positions. I will likely be adding capital toward some of these over the next several months.
